The current 16 files were all structural; no conversation framing applied this round.
  - **"observer" vs "admin":** per the rules doc both are approved replacements for "moderator". Used "admin" wherever the role is a HARD MODERATION GATE (flag review, civility-heuristic review, validation `needs_moderator_review`, gameStatus.needs_judge_human_review) — the function is operator review, not passive observation. Used the existing "observer" canon vocabulary nowhere new (it already covers the read-only role in OBSERVER_COPY).
  - **"Debate club" mode name:** chose "Practice argument club" over plain "Argument club" to retain the cultural "practice / structured-rehearsal" connotation the original implied, while replacing "debate" with "argument" per the canon vocabulary. Other mode names in ARGUMENT_MODE_COPY use varied vocabulary (e.g., "Friendly disagreement", "Workplace decision", "Evidence review") so adding "practice" fits the pattern.
  - **ArgumentTreeScreen empty-state wording:** the audit-flagged string was "This debate has no posted arguments." A literal replacement would yield "This argument has no posted arguments." — awkward repetition. Used the established "move" vocabulary ("This argument has no posted moves yet.") because COMPOSER_COPY.yourMove = "Your Move" and the entire MOVE_COPY / TIMELINE_COPY surface already treats "move" as the canonical word for a message inside a room. This keeps the noun for the room ("argument") distinct from the noun for the messages ("moves").
  - **Anthropic prompt labels (buildAnthropicRequest.ts + anthropicProvider.ts):** the prompt labels "Debate resolution:" / "Debate description:" / "User's debate side:" travel to the AI provider (not the user). Strictly speaking they're prompt content, not UI copy — but the audit's scope includes them (`src/features/` is in scope, supabase functions are NOT). Changed both the Node-side mirror AND the supabase function side to keep them in sync; the Node-side comment explicitly says "Mirrors the logic in supabase/functions/_shared/languageProcessing/anthropicProvider.ts (buildAnthropicRequestBody). Keep in sync if the prompt changes." The TypeScript field names `input.debateResolution` / `input.debateDescription` / `input.userSide` stay unchanged per the internal-identifier exemption.
  - **"Could not load debates" string in DebateListScreen:** the audit's JSX-text extractor uses `>[^<>{}]+<`, which skipped this string because it sits next to a `{error}` JSX expression. Not flagged in the audit table, but updated for consistency since the whole screen received the scrub.

- **+0 tests / +0 suites.** Full suite **9542 tests / 360 suites — all passing** (unchanged from the MCP-CAT-001 baseline of 9542 / 360). No test required updates because no existing test asserted the exact value of a modified user-facing string — the cascade tests scan ban-lists, not literal copy. Typecheck + lint clean.

- **Operator follow-up:** NONE. No migration. No Edge Function redeploy (the supabase-side prompt label change is included in this card's commit set, but the deployed `process-language-draft` Edge Function picks up the new prompt only when an operator redeploys it — the change is cosmetic-to-the-AI and not user-visible, so the redeploy is OPTIONAL). No secret change. No admin runtime-config change. Rollback: `git revert` the four QOL-035 commits — the audit returns to its 32 live + 7 legacy + 1 prohibited baseline. The audit script (`scripts/ux/auditUserFacingTerminology.js`) and the rules doc (`docs/ux-storyboards/terminology-and-copy-rules.md`) remain in force as the canonical reference for future copy.

## MCP-CAT-001 — Binary classifier catalog extension (Epic 12 — Rules UX)

**Status:** Build complete, awaiting Review — extends `SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG` (Node + Deno mirrors) from 23 to 35 ids. The 12 new ids surfaced by the band-space-rent evidence scenario (`fixtures/argument-scenarios/catalog-design-band-space-rent-evidence.json`) cover evidence applicability (disputes / debt markers / corroborating documents), qualified concessions (accept-with-caveat / alternate interpretation), structural patterns (prior agreement / temporal constraint / sub-axis introduction / paired concede-and-dispute), and settlement (proposes / accepts terms — the two settlement ids are operator-specified extensions whose structural questions were derived from the scenario's `expectedSettlement` section). Banner library extended with 24 new entries (4 primary + 4 soft for `evidence_debt`; 8 primary + 8 soft for `synthesis_readiness`). Reason-code safety whitelist extended with the new `settlement` family prefix. COMP-001's `R-CAT-SubAxis` runtime-guarded rule (un-stubbed now that `introduces_sub_axis` is in the catalog) plus five additional MCP-CAT-001 composition rules (`R-EV-APP-01`, `R-CAT-QualifiedConcession`, `R-CAT-Corroborating`, `R-CAT-Settlement` for both proposes/accepts) now fire for real on the new ids. Eight new `NodeVisualMutationType` enum values added (`evidence_applicability_disputed`, `prior_agreement_cited`, `temporal_constraint_provided`, `qualified_concession_with_caveat`, `alternate_interpretation_offered`, `corroborating_document_attached`, `settlement_proposed`, `settlement_accepted`). Three new batch groups (F, G, H) carry the 12 new ids while preserving `BATCH_CAP=5` and the partition property. Issue #238.

- **What it ships — new files:** `__tests__/mcpCat001NewClassifierIds.test.ts` (53 tests — every new id present in `ALL_SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_IDS`; catalog entry fields populated; structural-only "Does this move…?" convention; bannerCode + ledgerFeedbackCode resolve; binarySignal doctrine scan; settlement-id wording snapshot). `__tests__/mcpCat001ReasonCodeWhitelist.test.ts` (28 tests — `settlement` family in `REASON_CODE_FAMILIES`; existing eight families preserved; verdict / person-label scan still rejects malicious reason codes; settlement family does not bypass either ban-list; two settlement ids round-trip cleanly through `parseSemanticPacket`). `__tests__/mcpCat001CompositionRules.test.ts` (48 tests — per-rule activation snapshots: R-CAT-SubAxis fires + records SubAxisState; R-EV-APP-01 fires `evidence_applicability_disputed` on parent + `prior_agreement_cited` / `temporal_constraint_provided` chips on current; R-CAT-QualifiedConcession fires both `qualified_concession_with_caveat` + `alternate_interpretation_offered`; R-CAT-Corroborating fires `corroborating_document_attached`; R-CAT-Settlement fires both `settlement_proposed` + `settlement_accepted`; doctrine-clean scan over all MCP-CAT-001 mutation values).

- **What it ships — modified files:** `src/features/semanticReferee/semanticRefereeTypes.ts` (12 new ids added to `SemanticClassifierId` union + `ALL_SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_IDS`; `REASON_CODE_FAMILIES` gains `settlement`). `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/types.ts` (byte-mirror of the Node-side id union). `src/lib/constitution/semanticClassifierCatalog.ts` (12 new `SemanticClassifierCatalogEntry` literals appended in the order of the operator's authoritative list; settlement ids documented as operator extensions). `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/semanticClassifierCatalog.ts` (byte-identical Deno mirror of the new catalog entries). `src/features/refereeBanners/refereeBannerLibrary.ts` (24 new banner entries — 4 primary + 4 soft for evidence-applicability / debt-marker / corroborating-document banners; 8 primary + 8 soft for prior-agreement / temporal-anchor / qualified-concession / alternate-interpretation / sub-axis / concession-with-new-dispute / settlement-proposed / settlement-accepted banners). `src/features/arguments/gameCopy.ts` (24 new plain-language entries — every new bannerCode resolves to its headline; no snake_case leaks). `src/features/semanticReferee/classifierBatching.ts` (three new batch groups F, G, H carrying the 12 new ids; partition property preserved; `BATCH_CAP=5` unchanged; expected-batches-per-trigger still 2). `src/features/semanticReferee/compositionTypes.ts` (8 new `NodeVisualMutationType` enum values for the MCP-CAT-001 per-move structural markers; all added to `ALL_NODE_VISUAL_MUTATION_TYPES`). `src/features/semanticReferee/compositionLayer.ts` (R-CAT-SubAxis guard preserved as belt-and-suspenders; 5 new MCP-CAT-001 rules added — R-EV-APP-01 fires `evidence_applicability_disputed` on parent; R-CAT-QualifiedConcession; R-CAT-Corroborating; R-CAT-Settlement). `docs/architecture/semantic-referee-classifier-catalog.md` (header retitled "v1"; new §F section with 12 inventory sections for the new ids; findings extended with the 12 new "no-ledger-mapping" entries; `R-CAT-SubAxis` cross-referenced as the live composition rule that fires on `introduces_sub_axis`). `__tests__/semanticClassifierCatalogParity.test.ts` (length 23 → 35; with-cat 9, without-cat 14 → 26; null-ledger 12 → 24). `__tests__/semanticClassifierCatalogDenoNodeParity.test.ts` (length 23 → 35 across all per-id extractors). `__tests__/semanticAnthropicSeedPromptBanList.test.ts` (length 23 → 35). `__tests__/semanticRefereeClassifierCatalogParity.test.ts` (inventory-doc length 23 → 35). `__tests__/semanticBatching.test.ts` (5 groups A-E → 8 groups A-H; partition size 23 → 35; full-catalog batches 5 → 8). `__tests__/refereeBannerLibrary.test.ts` (library size 103 → 127). `__tests__/semanticBannerFuzzParity.test.ts` (frozen reference table extended with the 12 new ids' single-entry bannerCode lists). `__tests__/semanticPlainLanguageParity.test.ts` (frozen reference extended with 12 null entries for the new classifier ids — they map through bannerCode, not classifierId). `__tests__/semanticLedgerFuzzParity.test.ts` ("14 without ledgerCategories" → 26, with the 12 MCP-CAT-001 ids listed). `__tests__/compositionLayerBandSpaceRent.test.ts` (23-id mode filter is now a hardcoded literal of the catalog v0 ids — does not silently drift when the live catalog gains ids; 35-id mode test rewritten as a superset check with the R-CM-03 retarget on m8 documented as the one intentional difference). `docs/core/current-status.md` — this entry.

- **Doctrine encoded (cdiscourse-doctrine §1 / §4 / §6 / §9):** every new `structuralQuestion` asks about the move's STRUCTURE — never about truth, popularity, person, or outcome (the existing `semanticAnthropicSeedPromptBanList.test.ts` ban-list scan covers every new entry automatically because the test iterates the live catalog). Every new `binarySignal` is plain-language description of a structural property — verified by the new `mcpCat001NewClassifierIds.test.ts` doctrine scan. Every new `bannerCode` / headline / helper / accessibility label is doctrine-clean (the existing `refereeBannerBanList.test.ts` scans the whole library). Settlement copy explicitly follows the band-space-rent fixture's `permittedSettlementLanguage` (settled / resolved / accepted) and avoids the `forbiddenSettlementLanguage` (proven / true / false / winner / loser / case closed / right / wrong / correct / incorrect / victory / defeated) — asserted by a dedicated forbidden-token scan in the new id-coverage test. Every new `NodeVisualMutationType` value is scanned against the existing verdict + person-label ban-lists (`compositionLayerDoctrineScan.test.ts` already iterates the live `ALL_NODE_VISUAL_MUTATION_TYPES`). The new `settlement` reason-code family does NOT bypass the verdict / person-label scan (verified by `mcpCat001ReasonCodeWhitelist.test.ts` — `settlement_winner_takes_all` still fails). No service-role usage. No `.env` touched. No direct insert into `public.arguments`. No production app calls Anthropic / xAI / X API.

- **Hard constraints honored:** NO existing classifier id, structural question, banner code, or ledger code was modified — MCP-CAT-001 is additive only. `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_PACKET_VERSION` unchanged (`mcp-semantic-referee-v0`). `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` unchanged (`mcp-semantic-referee-prompt-v2` — the prompt template iterates the catalog automatically; the structural shape did NOT change, so a version bump is not warranted per MCP-MOD-005's design). No migration written. No `process-language-draft` touched. No admin Edge Function touched. No MCP adapter touched. The 12 new ids are POSITIONALLY APPENDED in the catalog (not interspersed), preserving the snapshot golden file `__tests__/__fixtures__/semanticClassifierCatalog/firstThreeIdsGolden.txt` unchanged.

- **Implementer decisions documented:**
  - **Settlement-id wording (operator-derived):** the design doc + fixture do NOT specify `proposes_settlement_terms` / `accepts_settlement_terms`. Per the MCP-CAT-001 task spec, the structural questions were chosen to (a) match the operator's "Does this move …?" convention; (b) describe RESOLUTION TERMS, not verdicts on truth; (c) avoid the `forbiddenSettlementLanguage` list from the fixture's `expectedSettlement` section. Final wording: `proposes_settlement_terms` → "Does this move propose a settlement summary or resolution terms the other participant could accept?"; `accepts_settlement_terms` → "Does this move accept a proposed settlement summary or resolution terms?".
  - **Composition-rule conservatism:** the COMP-001 worked examples document several 35-id-mode rule tightenings. Per the task spec's "conservative recommendation", this card adds only the rules clearly documented in the worked examples — R-CAT-SubAxis (un-stubbed), R-EV-APP-01 (with per-move chips for prior-agreement / temporal-constraint), R-CAT-QualifiedConcession (with per-move alternate-interpretation chip), R-CAT-Corroborating, R-CAT-Settlement (both proposes and accepts). The other proposed tightenings (e.g. making R-EV-01 require `asks_for_evidence=1 AND opens_evidence_debt_marker=1`) are deliberately DEFERRED — the existing rules produce identical output behavior on the catalog v1 ids in the worked examples (only the precondition would tighten), and the band-space-rent superset check confirms no regression. Two ids (`opens_evidence_debt_marker` / `closes_evidence_debt_marker`) have catalog entries + banner codes but no dedicated composition rule yet; they surface through the banner library only.
  - **Batching extension:** the design's §14 reviewer question #6 anticipated this — "with 12 new ids, batching for post-submit moments may need to grow to 3 batches instead of 2." Three new groups (F, G, H) carry 4 ids each (total 12), respecting `BATCH_CAP=5`. The expected-max-per-trigger constant (`EXPECTED_MAX_BATCHES_PER_TRIGGER = 2`) is unchanged — that constant documents the per-MOMENT expectation, not the per-CATALOG count.
  - **Banner-category reuse:** the 24 new banners fit into the existing `evidence_debt` (4 primary + 4 soft) and `synthesis_readiness` (8 primary + 8 soft) categories — no new `RefereeBannerCategory` value added (that would cascade to category-floor checks across the category-priority + selection tests). The category floors are minimums (≥), so adding banners to existing categories does NOT break any floor assertion.
  - **`SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` NOT bumped:** the prompt template iterates the catalog and emits one question line per requested classifier. Adding ids to the catalog grows the question list but does NOT change the template shape, the instruction text, the worked-example block, or the redacted-input block. Per MCP-MOD-005's design ("Bumped to `-v2` in MCP-MOD-008: `buildInputBlock` now emits an optional `Room thread context:` block… The structural format of the prompt changed"), a version bump is warranted when the prompt's STRUCTURAL format changes — not when the catalog grows. The snapshot test (`semanticClassifierCatalogSnapshotPrompt.test.ts`) passes unchanged because its sample requests the first three ids (`responds_to_parent`, `introduces_new_issue`, `asks_for_evidence`), all of which remain at position 0/1/2 in the catalog. No golden-file regeneration needed.

- **+151 tests / +3 suites.** Full suite **9542 tests / 360 suites — all passing** (from the 9391 / 357 baseline). Typecheck + lint + skills:validate clean.

- **Operator follow-up:** run `npx supabase functions deploy semantic-referee --linked` after merge to ship the catalog v1 entries on the Deno side (the new ids enter the inbound + outbound schemas via `ALL_SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_IDS`; the seed prompt picks them up automatically; the content-safety scan rejects malicious reason codes under the new `settlement` family). No migration. No secret change. No admin runtime-config change. The live Anthropic provider will start receiving requests for the new ids when MCP-002's per-moment requested-classifier policy is taught about them (a future card); until then, the existing per-moment policy continues to request the same catalog v0 ids and the new ids land dormant on the contract. Rollback: `git revert` the merge commit — the new ids disappear from both mirrors, the banner library drops the 24 new entries, the composition rules drop the new mutation types, and the catalog returns to its 23-id catalog v0 shape.

## COMP-001 — Deterministic composition layer for connected-node visual state (Epic 12 — Rules UX)

**Status:** Build complete, awaiting Review — pure-TS function `composeVisualState` maps the semantic referee's binary classifier signal vectors into typed `NodeVisualMutation` enum values targeting specific connected `moveId`s on the timeline. 22 composition rules + 2 exemption patterns (R-EX-01 root, R-EX-02 first-move-per-author) covering parent-continuity, evidence / source-chain, constructive movement, debate-mode, and branch / friction families. 12 PROPOSED ids from MCP-CAT-001 are referenced behind runtime catalog guards — the rules degrade gracefully today and automatically activate when MCP-CAT-001 lands the ids. The composition layer is PURE (no Supabase, no React, no network, no async, no AI SDK), deterministic (snapshot-testable), and produces ZERO natural language (typed enum values only; rendering is downstream via the existing `gameCopy.toPlainLanguage` pattern). Integration is ADDITIVE: `useSemanticReferee` gains `getMutationsForMove(moveId)` + `getCompositionState()` accessors with the existing public surface unchanged; `argumentGameSurfaceModel.ts` gains an optional `crossNodeMutations` field + `getCrossNodeMutations` helper for the bubble-rendering layer to consume in a future UI card. `scripts/bot-fixtures/runMcpSmokeTest.js` optionally logs mutations per move (diagnostic-only, non-gating). Issue #244.

- **What it ships — new files:** `src/features/semanticReferee/compositionTypes.ts` (pure-TS type contract — `CompositionState` + per-item lifecycle types `EvidenceDebtState` / `ClarificationDebtState` / `SubAxisState` / `ConcessionLinkState` / `SourceChainGapState` / `NarrowingLinkState` / `SynthesisReadinessState`; `NodeVisualMutationType` closed enum of 35 structural values; `NodeVisualMutation` shape with `targetMoveId` + `sourceClassifier` + optional `edgeOtherEndpointMoveId`; `MoveMetadata` + `AncestorMoveSummary` input types; `ComposeVisualStateInput` + `CompositionResult` function contract; `EMPTY_COMPOSITION_STATE` initial-value constant). `src/features/semanticReferee/compositionLayer.ts` (the pure function — 22 rules grouped by family with deterministic execution order: parent-continuity → evidence → constructive-movement → debate-mode → branch/friction; PROPOSED-id rules under runtime catalog guards via `classifierInCatalog`; same purity rules as `engine.ts`). `src/features/semanticReferee/compositionUpstreamSearch.ts` (the "walk up the chain" helper used by R-EV-02 / R-CM-01 / R-CM-02 / R-CM-03 / R-EV-06 / R-PC-04; convenience wrappers `findUpstreamBySameAuthor` + `findUpstreamByDifferentAuthor` with optional axis filter; exported so the smoke-test orchestrator + tests can import without coupling to the rule list). `__tests__/compositionLayerRules.test.ts` (31 tests — one per active rule + exemption pattern + multi-rule interaction asserting execution-order stability). `__tests__/compositionLayerBandSpaceRent.test.ts` (9 tests — 8-move scenario replay from `fixtures/argument-scenarios/catalog-design-band-space-rent-evidence.json` in 23-id mode; plus a 35-id forward-compat parity check asserting PROPOSED-id rules do NOT speculatively fire before MCP-CAT-001 lands). `__tests__/compositionLayerRemoteWorkProductivity.test.ts` (8 tests — 8-move scenario replay from `fixtures/argument-scenarios/smoke-test-mcp-remote-work-productivity.json`; uses ONLY current 23-id classifiers — the cleanest regression test). `__tests__/compositionLayerEdgeCases.test.ts` (9 tests — empty signal vector; all-1s vector; root; first-move-by-author; soft-deleted parent indifference; no-packet case; multiple open evidence debts; retraction-without-evidence; explicit mode-mismatch documentation). `__tests__/compositionLayerDoctrineScan.test.ts` (25 tests — every `NodeVisualMutationType` value passes the verdict + person-label ban-list scan; every mutation emitted across the all-ones packet + root exemption path also passes the scan). `__tests__/compositionLayerPurity.test.ts` (7 tests — composition module imports no forbidden network / UI / AI dependency; no `await` / `Promise` in the layer source; determinism check on repeat calls with the same input; immutability check on the input state; frozen `nextState` + `mutations` return). `__tests__/compositionUpstreamSearch.test.ts` (12 tests — undefined / empty chain; most-recent-match-wins; index-from-immediate-parent passed to predicate; 1000-deep chain without recursion issues; same-author / different-author / axis-filtered wrappers). `__tests__/useSemanticRefereeComposition.test.tsx` (6 tests — hook-level integration: the new `getMutationsForMove` + `getCompositionState` methods are additive; unknown move ids return empty arrays; the empty state is the starting value; mutations land in the lookup after a successful classify; state accumulates across multiple posted moves; the fallback path does NOT touch composition state).

- **What it ships — modified files:** `src/features/arguments/useSemanticReferee.ts` — adds COMP-001 imports; introduces `compositionStateRef` (`useRef<CompositionState>` initialized to `EMPTY_COMPOSITION_STATE`) + `mutationsByMoveIdRef` (in-memory index keyed by target `moveId`); extends `finalizeReady` to invoke `composeVisualState` after a successful classify (PURE / SYNC — no extra await), feeding the structural metadata + ancestor chain derived from `OnMovePostedArgs.authorId` + `priorMoves`; gates composition off when the result is a cache hit (already composed earlier in the session); exposes `getMutationsForMove(moveId) → readonly NodeVisualMutation[]` + `getCompositionState() → CompositionState` on the public surface — the existing methods are UNCHANGED. `src/features/arguments/argumentGameSurfaceModel.ts` — adds a type-only import of `NodeVisualMutation`; adds an OPTIONAL `crossNodeMutations?: ReadonlyMap<string, readonly NodeVisualMutation[]>` field to `ArgumentSurfaceState`; adds a `getCrossNodeMutations(state, moveId)` helper for the bubble-rendering layer to consume. The field is NOT wired into the bubble rendering yet — that's downstream UI cards (BR-001, VG-002, QOL-036 / QOL-037). `scripts/bot-fixtures/runMcpSmokeTest.js` — guards `require()` of the composition layer with try / catch (Node cannot import the .ts file without a build artifact — the orchestrator continues to work and composition logging is skipped); after each classify call, if the layer was successfully imported, the script feeds the merged packet + accumulated state + move metadata to `composeVisualState` and records the resulting mutations on each `moveLog`. The smoke test's exit code is UNCHANGED — composition is additive, NOT a gate. `__tests__/useSemanticReferee.test.ts` — the public-surface-keys assertion is updated to include the two new additive accessors (`getCompositionState`, `getMutationsForMove`). `docs/core/current-status.md` — this entry.

- **Doctrine encoded (cdiscourse-doctrine §1 / §2 / §3 / §4):** the layer makes NO truth claims — every value is a STRUCTURAL state (debt opened, point conceded, sub-axis resolved); the composition function reads 0/1 values from `SemanticBinarySample.value` and does NOT interpret `reasonCode` strings (the binary contract is preserved); the `popularity_amplification_warning` rule (R-EV-04) emits ONLY on the current move — doctrine forbids attaching a verdict to an ancestor; every value in `NodeVisualMutationType` is scanned against the verdict ban-list (`winner` / `loser` / `truth` / `correct` / `wrong` / `right` / `false` / `proven` / `defeated` / `won` / `lost`) and the person-label ban-list (`liar` / `dishonest` / `bad faith` / `manipulative` / `extremist` / `propagandist` / `troll`) — zero hits across all 35 enum values + all emitted mutations under the all-ones packet; the layer produces ZERO natural language (rendering is downstream); the layer is PURE TS (same purity rules as `engine.ts` — no Supabase / React / network / async / AI SDK imports — enforced by `compositionLayerPurity.test.ts`); the layer is DETERMINISTIC (same inputs → same outputs — enforced by the determinism test in the purity suite); the layer is INDIFFERENT to deletion state (the design's §13 Q4 — rendering policy lives downstream); the layer does NOT decide what the AI classifies (the catalog is the source of truth for classifier ids — adding an id is MCP-CAT-001 territory; adding a rule is COMP-001 territory; the two slates do not collide).

- **Implementer decisions documented:**
  - **35-id mode tests (band-space-rent):** included as a forward-compatibility parity check that asserts the runtime-guarded PROPOSED-id rules do NOT speculatively fire before MCP-CAT-001 lands the ids in the catalog (the 35-id snapshot equals the 23-id snapshot). A test that verifies the PROPOSED-rule outputs once MCP-CAT-001 ships belongs in MCP-CAT-001's implementation card, not here.
  - **Hook-level integration test:** included per the doctrine review's non-blocking suggestion #1. `__tests__/useSemanticRefereeComposition.test.tsx` posts 2 moves through the hook with mocked `classifyMove`, asserts the mutations accumulate in `getMutationsForMove`, and asserts the fallback path (classify returned `{ enabled: false }`) does NOT touch composition state.
  - **PROPOSED-id runtime guards:** the 12 PROPOSED ids named are `disputes_evidence_applicability`, `opens_evidence_debt_marker`, `closes_evidence_debt_marker`, `introduces_sub_axis`, `concedes_with_new_dispute`, `supplies_corroborating_document`, `references_prior_agreement`, `provides_temporal_constraint`, `accepts_partial_with_caveat`, `provides_alternate_interpretation`, `disputes_specific_amount`, `cites_temporal_boundary`. Only `introduces_sub_axis` has an explicit guarded rule wired in this card (it would emit `sub_axis_opened` + update `activeSubAxes` when present); the remaining 11 are documented in the file header as forward-compat seams. Each call site to a PROPOSED id is gated by `classifierInCatalog(id)` so the rule skips cleanly until MCP-CAT-001 declares the id.
  - **File location:** the design's §10 named `src/features/composition/composeVisualState.ts`. Per the implementer task's spec the modules live under `src/features/semanticReferee/` (alongside `semanticRefereeTypes.ts` and `triggerGates.ts`) so the catalog import path is short and the layer travels with its upstream contract.
  - **Smoke-test orchestrator coupling:** the Node `require()` of the .ts file fails at runtime without a transpile step. The orchestrator's import is guarded with try / catch — composition logging is skipped when the import fails; the smoke test continues to work as before. A future card may add a build step that emits a JS bundle for the runner, or port `composeVisualState` to a JS twin (the parallel pattern used for `clientRedaction` + `classifierBatching`).
  - **Synthesis-readiness sharedGroundMoveCount:** the design's §3.2 declares the field; the current implementation always returns the accumulator's previous value (no rule increments it). A future card may wire the count from R-CM-02 + R-CM-03 fires, or from a richer "shared ground detected" classifier (post-MCP-CAT-001).

- **+120 tests / +8 suites.** Full suite **9391 tests / 357 suites — all passing** (from the 9271 / 349 baseline). Typecheck + lint + skills:validate clean.

- **Operator follow-up:** NONE. The composition layer is pure-TS and entirely additive on top of the existing MCP-019 hook. No Edge Function redeploy is required (no Deno mirror change, no `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_PACKET_VERSION` bump, no `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` bump, no schema change, no migration, no secret change, no admin runtime-config change). The smoke test's exit code is unchanged. Composition mutations will start surfacing in the UI when the downstream cards (BR-001 point-lifecycle states, VG-002 gradient wave rail, QOL-036 / QOL-037 evidence-object UI) wire `getMutationsForMove` into the bubble-rendering code. Rollback is `git revert` of the merge commit — the three new files disappear, `useSemanticReferee` reverts to its prior public surface (the new methods drop off), and `argumentGameSurfaceModel.ts`'s optional `crossNodeMutations` field disappears (no consumers depend on it yet).

## MCP-MOD-008 — Move-position-aware triggering rule + full-thread context (Epic 12 — Rules UX · Movement C)

**Status:** Build complete, awaiting Review — **FINAL CARD of the modularity slate**. Movement C, Card 2 of 2. **The ONLY card in the entire slate that changes user-observable behavior.** Stage 6.4.x — From each participant's SECOND move onward, the semantic referee evaluates the FULL thread context, not just the parent. Each participant's FIRST move in a debate no longer triggers classification (it would mostly fail by definition). Visible effect: users see the referee banner on their second move and onward; the layer-1 fallback is what they see on the opening statement and first rebuttal, exactly as if `{ enabled: false }` came back. The first-move-skip is participant-scoped — a chime-in participant's first contribution is exempt too, automatic from the pure helper's counting logic. Issue #237.

- **What it ships — new files:** `src/features/semanticReferee/threadContext.ts` (pure-TS helpers `buildAuthorAliasMap` + `assemblePriorMovesPayload` + `aliasForIndex`; spreadsheet-column alias scheme A..Z, AA..AZ, BA..BZ; redacts each prior-move body via the client redactor, drops moves whose author has no alias, enforces the per-batch token budget by dropping OLDEST first, returns [] when even move + parent overflow); `__tests__/triggerGateFirstMoveSkip.test.ts` (15 tests — root move / first-by-opponent / first-by-chime-in all refuse with `first_move_by_author`; A's second + later moves allowed; ordering: refusal runs BEFORE per-moment preconditions but AFTER layer / mode / role checks; backward compat when authorId / priorMoves are absent); `__tests__/movePositionThreadBudget.test.ts` (11 tests — under / just-over / way-over budget regimes; oldest-first drop preserving newest; budget accounting for prior-move bytes; defensive paths); `__tests__/seedPromptThreadContextBlock.test.ts` (10 tests — block presence / absence / ordering above parent + move blocks; chronological preservation; byte-for-byte `- Move N by <alias>: <body>` 1-indexed format; AA wrap-around alias support; v2 prompt-version assertion); `__tests__/classifyMoveRequestPriorMovesParity.test.ts` (15 tests — source-scan parity across Node `edgeFunctions.ts`, Deno `types.ts`, Deno `schema.ts`, Deno `redaction.ts` for the new optional field shape; alias bound `MAX_ALIAS_LEN`; field-name agreement; PII-field absence on `PriorMoveContext`); `__tests__/movePositionRoomHookIntegration.test.ts` (8 tests — synthetic 5-move room; A1 / B1 / C-chime-in first moves all skip; A's second + B's second fire WITH the redacted prior-moves payload; alias stability across calls; no raw user id / display name / email leaks; backward-compat path when priorMoves is absent).
- **What it ships — modified files:** `src/features/semanticReferee/triggerGates.ts` — `TriggerEvaluationInput` gains optional `authorId?: string` + `priorMoves?: ReadonlyArray<{ id; authorId }>`; `TriggerReasonCode` gains `'first_move_by_author'`; `evaluateTrigger` consults `getMovePositionForAuthor` after the role check and before per-moment preconditions; the check engages only when BOTH `authorId` and `priorMoves` are supplied (backward compat preserved). `src/features/semanticReferee/tokenBudget.ts` — `TokenBudgetPayload` gains optional `priorMoveBodies?: ReadonlyArray<string>`; `estimatePacketTokens` adds prior-move bytes + `PER_PRIOR_MOVE_SCAFFOLD_CHARS = 24` per entry. `src/features/arguments/useSemanticReferee.ts` — `OnMovePostedArgs` gains optional `authorId` + `priorMoves`; `onMovePosted` derives an alias map ONCE per call, assembles the per-batch `priorMovesRedacted` payload via the threadContext helpers, sends it on the classify call when non-empty; passes `authorId` + `priorMoves` to `evaluateTrigger` when both are present. `src/features/arguments/ArgumentTreeScreen.tsx` — the wire-in: assembles `priorMoves` from the chronological `rows`, passes `justPosted.authorId` + the prior-moves list to `refereeOnMovePosted`. `src/lib/edgeFunctions.ts` — `ClassifyMoveRequest` gains optional `priorMovesRedacted?: ReadonlyArray<PriorMoveContext>`; new `PriorMoveContext` interface (`authorAlias: string` + `bodyRedacted: string`). `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/types.ts` — Deno-side mirror of `PriorMoveContext` + the new optional field on `ClassifyMoveRequest`. `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/schema.ts` — new `PriorMoveContextSchema` (`.strict()`, `authorAlias` bounded by `MAX_ALIAS_LEN = 8`, `bodyRedacted` bounded by `MOVE_BODY_MAX`); `ClassifyMoveRequestSchema` gains the optional `priorMovesRedacted` array bounded by `MAX_PRIOR_MOVES = 50`. `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/redaction.ts` — `redactClassifyMoveRequest` now runs `redactString` over each `priorMovesRedacted[].bodyRedacted` (belt-and-suspenders, same posture as `moveBodyRedacted` + `parentBodyRedacted`); `authorAlias` is NOT redacted (it is non-identifying by construction and length-bounded by the schema). `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/seedPrompt.ts` — `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` bumped `v1 → v2` (structural format change); `buildInputBlock` emits a new `Room thread context (most recent move is the one to classify):` block ABOVE the parent + move blocks when `priorMovesRedacted` is non-empty; each entry formatted as `- Move N by <alias>: <redacted body>` 1-indexed; absent / empty `priorMovesRedacted` collapses the prompt to the pre-MCP-MOD-008 shape byte-for-byte. `__tests__/semanticRouterBanList.test.ts` — adds `'first_move_by_author'` to `ALL_TRIGGER_REASON_CODES` (documentation-only; the new code passes the existing ban-list scan — the token `first` is not banned). `__tests__/semanticAnthropicCore.test.ts` — updates the `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` assertion from `v1` to `v2`. `__tests__/semanticRefereeSeedPromptEnumCoverage.test.ts` — updates the `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` source-text assertion from `v1` to `v2`. `docs/core/current-status.md` — this entry.
- **Doctrine encoded:** the alias map is STABLE and NON-IDENTIFYING — chronological order of distinct authors maps to A, B, C, ..., Z, AA, AB, ...; no user id, no display name, no email crosses the boundary; the boundary schema bounds `authorAlias` length (`MAX_ALIAS_LEN = 8`); the system prompt already prohibits person-labeling; the integration test asserts no raw user id leaks into the priorMovesRedacted entries. The token-budget gate drops OLDEST entries first when over budget; way-over budget returns the empty array and the hook falls back to the pre-MCP-MOD-008 payload shape (graceful degradation; the hook does NOT skip the call). `priorMovesRedacted` is OPTIONAL in the request schema — existing callers (smoke-test orchestrator, fixtures) that do not send it continue to work unchanged. The first-move-skip engages only when the caller supplies both `authorId` + `priorMoves`; existing exhaustive trigger-gate tests that don't supply them keep working unchanged (backward compat). The chime-in's first contribution is automatically exempt (participant-scoped via `getMovePositionForAuthor`). `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_PACKET_VERSION` is unchanged (the response contract is unchanged); `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` is bumped v1 → v2 (the prompt format changed). No service-role usage. No direct insert. No new dependency. No migration. No env var.
- **Implementer decisions documented:** (1) **Feature flag (design §9.2): SKIPPED.** The design recommended an optional `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_FIRST_MOVE_SKIP` flag, but the first-move-skip engages only when the hook passes `authorId` + `priorMoves` to the gate. The operational rollback lever is to revert the hook wiring in `ArgumentTreeScreen.tsx` (stop passing the new args) — a single-file change that fully restores pre-MCP-MOD-008 behavior without a code revert. A flag would require non-trivial wiring (option on `useSemanticReferee`, branch in `onMovePosted`, two-state test coverage). Rollback path #1 (revert merge) remains fully sufficient. (2) **`SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` bump: YES (v1 → v2).** `buildInputBlock` now emits a new structural block when `priorMovesRedacted` is present. Even though the block is conditional, the structural format changed, so the version bumps and the upstream cache invalidates cleanly. (3) **Smoke-test orchestrator update: SKIPPED.** The orchestrator (`runMcpSmokeTest.js`) bypasses `evaluateTrigger` and calls `invokeClassifyMove` directly, so behavior is unchanged. Wiring it to send `priorMovesRedacted` would require non-trivial work (alias tracking, chronological list, redaction, budget trim) and would not change the smoke-test outcome (16/16 still classify). The boundary parity tests already prove the field is honored on both sides; `movePositionRoomHookIntegration.test.ts` exercises the end-to-end shape. Documented as a future enhancement (orchestrator parity with the hook).
- **Behavior change tests updated (called out for reviewer sign-off):** (i) `semanticRouterBanList.test.ts` — added `'first_move_by_author'` to `ALL_TRIGGER_REASON_CODES`; the ban-list scan continues to pass (the token `first` is not on the verdict / person ban-list); behavior change: the gate now has 15 reason codes instead of 14. (ii) `semanticAnthropicCore.test.ts` and `semanticRefereeSeedPromptEnumCoverage.test.ts` — `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` assertion bumped from `v1` to `v2`; behavior change: the upstream packet cache invalidates on first redeploy because the prompt format changed (a new `Room thread context` block is emitted when `priorMovesRedacted` is present). No existing trigger-gate behavior assertion needed to flip (the existing tests don't supply `authorId` / `priorMoves` to the gate, so backward compat preserves their semantics unchanged).
- **+59 tests / +5 suites.** Full suite **9271 tests / 349 suites — all passing** (from the 9212 / 344 baseline). Typecheck + lint + skills:validate clean.
- **Operator follow-up:** run `npx supabase functions deploy semantic-referee --linked` after merge to ship the new optional `priorMovesRedacted` field (Deno schema + types + redaction + seedPrompt v2). Then re-run the smoke test (`node scripts/bot-fixtures/runMcpSmokeTest.js`) and confirm 16/16 moves classified on both passes (anthropic + mock), 0 errors, `binaries=9` on every move — IDENTICAL to pre-MCP-MOD-008 (the orchestrator bypasses the gate and does not send `priorMovesRedacted`). No migration, no secret change, no admin runtime-config change. Rollback path #1: `git revert` the merge commit — the new optional field disappears from the schema, the gate stops consulting the helper, the prompt collapses to v1. Rollback path #2: revert the wire-in line in `ArgumentTreeScreen.tsx` (stop passing `authorId` + `priorMoves` to `refereeOnMovePosted`) — pre-MCP-MOD-008 behavior fully restored without a code revert, the field stays defined-but-unused on the schema.

## MCP-MOD-007 — Move-position tracking helper (Epic 12 — Rules UX · Movement C)

**Status:** Build complete, awaiting Review — Movement C, Card 1 of 2. **No behavior change in this card.** Adds a small, PURE helper `getMovePositionForAuthor` that derives whether a just-posted (or about-to-be-posted) move is the FIRST, SECOND, or LATER move by its author in the debate. Pure-TS, no Supabase, no React, no async, no network — same purity rules as `engine.ts`. The classification is participant-scoped: it counts how many entries in `priorMoves` share the input's `authorId` (0 → `'first'`, 1 → `'second'`, ≥2 → `'later'`). Chime-in handling is automatic: a chime-in participant's first contribution returns `'first'` regardless of how many moves the primary participants have made before them. The helper does NOT talk to Supabase — the caller (MCP-MOD-008) is responsible for assembling `priorMoves` from the room state, and for NOT including the move under classification inside `priorMoves`. **No file outside `src/features/semanticReferee/movePosition.ts` and its test file imports the helper yet** — MCP-MOD-008 wires it into `evaluateTrigger`. **No change to `triggerGates.ts`, `seedPrompt.ts`, the banner library, the ledger, the content-safety scan, the catalog file, any Edge Function, `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_PACKET_VERSION`, or any schema.** Issue #236.

- **What it ships — new files:** `src/features/semanticReferee/movePosition.ts` (pure-TS helper; exports `MovePosition` type, `MovePositionInput` interface, and `getMovePositionForAuthor` function with early short-circuit at count ≥ 2); `__tests__/movePositionHelper.test.ts` (12 tests — 7 core counting-contract tests covering first / second / later transitions, ignoring other authors, empty `priorMoves`, the moveId-in-priorMoves edge case; plus 5 scenario tests covering first-move-by-initiator with empty `priorMoves`, first-move-by-opponent after initiator's root, second-move-by-initiator after a reply, first-move-by-chime-in after 5 primary-participant moves, and an author with zero prior moves in a 20-move thread by others).
- **What it ships — modified files:** `docs/core/current-status.md` — this entry.
- **Doctrine encoded:** the helper performs counting only — it never produces a verdict, a truth value, a winner / loser label, or any person-label of any kind. Pure TypeScript with same purity rules as `engine.ts` (no Supabase / React / network imports, side-effect-free, JSON-serializable inputs / outputs). No file outside the helper itself and its test file imports it yet — MCP-MOD-008's diff is the wire-in.
- **+12 tests / +1 suite.** Full suite **9212 tests / 344 suites — all passing** (from the 9200 / 343 baseline). Typecheck + lint + skills:validate clean.
- **Operator follow-up:** none. The helper is pure-TS and unused; no Edge Function redeploy, no migration, no secret change, no admin runtime-config change. MCP-MOD-008 wires the helper into `evaluateTrigger`. Rollback is `git revert` of the merge commit — the helper file disappears and no caller depended on it.

## MCP-MOD-006 — Banner + ledger refactor: `CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS` and `classifierFor` consume `SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG` (Epic 12 — Rules UX · Movement B)

**Status:** Build complete, awaiting Review — the **final card of Movement B** in the semantic-referee modularity slate. Closes the two documented departures MCP-MOD-004 reviewer-approved (PASS 21/21): the banner library's `CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS` and the ledger's inverted `classifierFor` table are now DERIVED VIEWS over the catalog. Extends `SemanticClassifierCatalogEntry` (Node + Deno mirrors stay byte-identical) with two new fields: `bannerCodePriorityList: readonly string[]` (the full ordered candidate list per id — `selectBanner` consumes this; the primary `bannerCode` is its first entry) and `ledgerCategories: readonly string[]` (the list of `RefereePointCategory` values the id surfaces under in the ledger's `l2SignalForCategory` lookup — inverted to build the `category → id` table at module load; `responds_to_parent` is the only id that surfaces under two categories: `continuity` AND `direct_response`). `toPlainLanguage` (in `src/features/arguments/gameCopy.ts`) now consults `CATALOG_BY_ID.get(id).plainLanguageLabel` first and falls back to the existing `PLAIN_LANGUAGE_COPY` table — catalog v0 has no label set on any of the 23 entries so behavior is byte-identical. **Behavior is byte-identical** — the MCP-MOD-004 golden snapshot test still passes byte-for-byte, three new fuzz-parity tests confirm the post-refactor and pre-refactor reference implementations agree across 50 random packets (banner), 50 random packets × 10 categories (ledger), and every classifier id (plain-language). **No new classifier ids, no banner codes, no ledger codes, no `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_PACKET_VERSION` bump, no `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` bump, no `seedPrompt.ts` change, no `process-language-draft` change, no admin Edge Function change, no MCP adapter change, no migration, no env var.** Issue #235.

- **What it ships — new files:** `__tests__/semanticBannerFuzzParity.test.ts` (6 tests — 50 deterministically-random `BannerSelectionInput` packets seeded with `COMMITTED_SEED = 0x6dcb5106`; for each fixture asserts the live `selectBanner` and a local-only reference impl that reads a frozen pre-MCP-MOD-006 `CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS` literal produce byte-identical `BannerSelectionResult`s; plus corpus shape, determinism, and a sanity assertion that the reference is a distinct function object); `__tests__/semanticLedgerFuzzParity.test.ts` (10 tests — asserts the catalog-derived `classifierFor` view equals the frozen pre-MCP-MOD-006 literal entry-for-entry; for 50 random packets × 10 categories asserts the live l2-signal helper and a local-only reference impl that reads the frozen literal produce identical `(present, sign, confidence)` tuples; plus inversion-collision-freedom and the 14-without-lookup catalog v0 baseline; seeded with `COMMITTED_SEED = 0x4f6d8c12`); `__tests__/semanticPlainLanguageParity.test.ts` (8 tests — for every classifier id in `ALL_SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_IDS` asserts `toPlainLanguage(id)` returns the captured pre-MCP-MOD-006 value; plus the catalog-v0 baseline that no entry sets `plainLanguageLabel`, the override-precedence shape, and the non-classifier fallback path).
- **What it ships — modified files:** `src/lib/constitution/semanticClassifierCatalog.ts` (Node-side catalog) and `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/semanticClassifierCatalog.ts` (Deno-side mirror) — `SemanticClassifierCatalogEntry` gains two new fields `bannerCodePriorityList` + `ledgerCategories`; every existing entry is populated (the per-id priority list lifted verbatim from the pre-refactor `CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS` block in `classifierBannerMap.ts`; the per-id category list lifted verbatim from the pre-refactor inline `classifierFor` block in `reconcileMove.l2SignalForCategory`); header docstrings updated. `src/features/refereeBanners/classifierBannerMap.ts` — `CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS` becomes a single `Object.fromEntries(SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG.map(...))` derivation; the 23 hand-written per-id list entries are gone; header docstring updated to name the new authority chain. `src/features/refereeLedger/reconcileMove.ts` — the inline `classifierFor` `const` inside `l2SignalForCategory` is replaced with a module-level `CLASSIFIER_FOR_CATEGORY` table built at module load by inverting the catalog's `ledgerCategories` field (defensively guards against same-category-twice collisions — the build throws if catalog v0 ever introduces a collision); `l2SignalForCategory`'s body reads the derived table. `src/features/arguments/gameCopy.ts` — `toPlainLanguage` now consults the catalog's `plainLanguageLabel` first; a new private `catalogPlainLanguageLabelFor` helper handles the lookup; the existing fallback to `PLAIN_LANGUAGE_COPY` is preserved. `__tests__/semanticClassifierCatalogParity.test.ts` — the now-tautological "bannerCode matches CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS[id][0]" assertion is rewritten as the catalog-internal invariant `bannerCode === bannerCodePriorityList[0]`; new `ledger-categories parity` block adds 4 assertions covering the type, inversion-collision-freedom, the 9-vs-14 catalog v0 split, and `responds_to_parent`'s two-category surface; new "every code in any bannerCodePriorityList is a valid banner code" + "derived view equals catalog" + "intentionally-silent classifiers have empty lists" assertions. `__tests__/semanticClassifierCatalogDenoNodeParity.test.ts` — two new extractors (`extractBannerPriorityLists`, `extractLedgerCategoriesLists`) and two new tests assert per-id parity between Node and Deno mirrors for the new fields. `__tests__/refereeBannerForbiddenImports.test.ts` — the allowlist for cross-feature imports adds `../../lib/constitution/semanticClassifierCatalog` (the new runtime import required for the derived view); the "type-only imports" sub-test docstring is updated to note the catalog is a runtime import and is correctly skipped. `docs/core/current-status.md` — this entry.
- **Doctrine encoded:** the catalog stays pure TypeScript — same `engine.ts` purity rules (no Supabase / React / network imports, frozen at module load, JSON-serializable values). `ledgerCategories` is typed as `readonly string[]` (the same posture `bannerCodePriorityList` already takes) so the catalog stays standalone-pure (no `import type { RefereePointCategory }` from the features tree) — the parity test enforces every value is a member of `ALL_REFEREE_POINT_CATEGORIES`. The Deno mirror's type matches the Node side byte-for-byte on the catalog span (no `import type`-shaped drift). `selectBanner` and `l2SignalForCategory` keep their pre-existing pure-TS character — the only changes are the table source (catalog-derived) and not the iteration / confidence / priority logic. `toPlainLanguage` keeps its plain-language contract: no internal codes ever reach a user surface; unknown codes are suppressed, not echoed. The MCP-013 anti-amplification doctrine is unchanged — engagement credit and factual-standing eligibility stay on separate axes.
- **Behavior preservation evidence:** (1) The MCP-MOD-004 golden snapshot test (`semanticClassifierCatalogSnapshotPrompt.test.ts`) still passes byte-for-byte — this card doesn't touch the prompt. (2) The banner fuzz-parity test compares the live `selectBanner` (consuming the catalog-derived `CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS`) against a local-only reference impl that reads a frozen pre-refactor literal table; 50/50 fixtures produce byte-identical `BannerSelectionResult`s. (3) The ledger fuzz-parity test asserts the catalog-derived `classifierFor` view equals the frozen pre-refactor literal entry-for-entry, AND for 50 random packets × 10 categories the live and reference l2-signal helpers produce identical `(present, sign, confidence)` tuples. (4) The plain-language parity test pins `toPlainLanguage(id)` to its pre-refactor return value for every classifier id (22 `null`s + the single `'Ready for synthesis'` for `ready_for_synthesis` that came from the pre-existing `PLAIN_LANGUAGE_COPY` table). All reference implementations live ONLY inside their respective test files as `localOnly*` functions; they never leak into the public API.
- **Catalog extension decisions (documented):** the design's §9 explicitly authorized extending `SemanticClassifierCatalogEntry` if the existing shape couldn't hold the per-id structure. Two fields were added: (i) `bannerCodePriorityList: readonly string[]` because `selectBanner` needs the FULL ordered list per id (not just the primary code) to pool candidates inside a category; (ii) `ledgerCategories: readonly string[]` because the ledger's lookup goes `RefereePointCategory → SemanticClassifierId` (the inverse direction of the catalog's `id → ledgerFeedbackCode`), AND a single id may surface under multiple categories (`responds_to_parent` for `continuity` + `direct_response`). Both fields are typed `readonly string[]` (matching the existing posture of `bannerCode: string | null`) so the catalog stays standalone-pure — the per-id parity tests enforce the value sets. Alternative B (keep the library tables, cross-reference the catalog) was rejected because it leaves two sources of truth and undermines the modularity goal; alternative C (drop `bannerCode` / `ledgerFeedbackCode` entirely) was rejected because the primary fields are still useful for downstream UI surfaces that want just the one canonical code per id.
- **+29 tests / +3 suites.** Full suite **9200 tests / 343 suites — all passing** (from the 9171 / 340 baseline). Typecheck + lint + skills:validate clean.
- **Operator follow-up:** the diff is entirely client-side (`src/` + the Deno-side catalog mirror only); the Deno-side `semanticClassifierCatalog.ts` change is consumed by `seedPrompt.ts` and the MCP / Anthropic providers via the existing catalog import path, so `npx supabase functions deploy semantic-referee --linked` after merge is still useful to ship the Deno mirror change. **The smoke test (`node scripts/bot-fixtures/runMcpSmokeTest.js`) is the structural regression check** — re-run it post-deploy and confirm results match the pre-MCP-MOD-006 baseline (the unit + fuzz-parity tests are the behavioural check; the smoke test is the contract-end-to-end check). No migration, no secret change, no admin runtime-config change. Rollback is `git revert` of the merge commit — the pre-refactor inline tables in `classifierBannerMap.ts` and `reconcileMove.ts` were not preserved verbatim, but every datum needed to reconstruct them lives in the catalog (and the local-only reference impls in the new fuzz-parity tests hold a frozen copy of each).

## MCP-MOD-005 — Prompt template refactor: `buildClassifierPrompt` iterates `SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG` (Epic 12 — Rules UX · Movement B)

**Status:** Build complete, awaiting Review — closes the MCP-MOD-004 source-of-truth handshake. `seedPrompt.ts`'s `buildClassifierPrompt` now iterates `SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG` directly and reads each entry's `structuralQuestion` field; the `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` export and its `Object.fromEntries(...)` derivation are removed. Adding or removing a classifier is now a one-line catalog edit — the prompt template never needs to be touched again unless the PROMPT STRUCTURE itself changes (the strict-JSON instruction, the worked example, or the input block). **Behavior is byte-identical** — the MCP-MOD-004 golden snapshot test (`semanticClassifierCatalogSnapshotPrompt.test.ts`) still passes byte-for-byte, the new 50-subset fuzz-parity test confirms the new function and a local reference implementation produce identical output across random catalog-ordered subsets, and the new iteration-order test pins the catalog declaration order as the output order. **No new classifier ids, no `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_PACKET_VERSION` bump, no `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` bump** (the prompt's text content is unchanged — only the assembly mechanism is refactored), **no Anthropic provider change, no inbound / outbound schema change, no admin runtime config change, no MCP adapter change, no migration, no env var.** Issue #234.

- **What it ships — new files:** `__tests__/semanticPromptBuildClassifierFuzzParity.test.ts` (3 tests — 50-subset byte-identity fuzz against a local-only legacy reference, stable repeated invocation, requested-set-only emission across every fuzz subset; seeded with `RNG_SEED = 0xc0ffee_05` committed in the file); `__tests__/semanticPromptBuildClassifierIterationOrder.test.ts` (5 tests — catalog order when all ids requested in catalog order, catalog order when all ids requested in REVERSE order (iteration source is the catalog), catalog id list parity with `ALL_SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_IDS`, contiguous catalog subsequence order preservation, de-duplication keeps catalog order when caller repeats ids).
- **What it ships — modified files:** `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/seedPrompt.ts` — `buildClassifierPrompt` now iterates `SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG` directly (`for (const entry of SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG) { if (!requestedIds.has(entry.id) || seen.has(entry.id)) continue; ... }`); the `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` export and its `Object.fromEntries(SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG.map(...))` derivation are deleted; the header docstring is updated to name the new authority chain; the `buildClassifierPrompt` JSDoc names the iteration-order property and the test file that pins it. `__tests__/_helpers/semanticRefereeDeno.ts` — the `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` re-export is removed; new `DENO_SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG` + `DENO_CATALOG_BY_ID` re-exports surface the Deno catalog mirror to tests that previously routed through the lookup table. `__tests__/semanticAnthropicCore.test.ts` — switched two `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT.<id>` references to a local `questionFor(id)` helper that reads from `DENO_CATALOG_BY_ID`. `__tests__/semanticAnthropicSeedPromptBanList.test.ts` — three id-coverage assertions and the per-question ban-list scan now read from `DENO_CATALOG_BY_ID` directly; the test still asserts the same SEMANTIC properties (every id has a non-empty structural question; no extra ids; every question is ban-list clean). `__tests__/semanticClassifierCatalogParity.test.ts` — the "structuralQuestion matches CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT" forward-compat insurance assertion (which became tautological after MCP-MOD-004 made the lookup catalog-derived) is replaced with "every catalog entry exposes a non-empty structuralQuestion (the catalog is the source of truth)"; the `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` import is removed. `__tests__/semanticRefereeClassifierCatalogParity.test.ts` — the doc-vs-prompt parity check now compares the doc's per-id `<!-- ai-question:<id> -->` block against `DENO_CATALOG_BY_ID.get(id)?.structuralQuestion` (the catalog is the source). `__tests__/seedPromptNoHardcodedClassifierIds.test.ts` — the "derives `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` from the catalog" assertion (matching `Object.fromEntries` + `SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG.map(...)`) is replaced with "iterates `SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG` directly" (matches `for (const entry of SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG)`) AND "does NOT export `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT`". `__tests__/semanticRefereeSeedPromptEnumCoverage.test.ts` — one stale comment that named `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` as a string-literal scan target is updated to name "the catalog-iteration line in `buildClassifierPrompt`" (no executable code change). `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/semanticClassifierCatalog.ts` and `src/lib/constitution/semanticClassifierCatalog.ts` — header JSDoc and `structuralQuestion` field docstrings updated to reflect the post-MCP-MOD-005 authority chain (no executable code change in either catalog file). `docs/architecture/semantic-referee-prompt-template.md` — the per-id question source-of-truth attribution, the §3 assembly description, and the §5 source-file map / invariants rows now name `SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG[].structuralQuestion` instead of `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT`. `docs/core/current-status.md` — this entry.
- **Doctrine encoded:** the catalog remains the sole source of truth for per-id structural questions; `buildClassifierPrompt` reads no per-id lookup table. Every `structuralQuestion` still asks about the move's STRUCTURE — never about truth, popularity, person, or outcome (the ban-list test now scans the catalog directly and continues to pass). The rules engine purity rules apply: `seedPrompt.ts` is pure TypeScript with zero `npm:` imports, the `_helpers/semanticRefereeDeno.ts` Jest bridge still `require()`s it directly. No live AI provider call by Claude in this session.
- **Behavior preservation evidence:** (1) The MCP-MOD-004 golden snapshot test (`semanticClassifierCatalogSnapshotPrompt.test.ts`) passes byte-for-byte against `__tests__/__fixtures__/semanticClassifierCatalog/firstThreeIdsGolden.txt` — same input produces the same output. (2) The new 50-subset fuzz-parity test (`semanticPromptBuildClassifierFuzzParity.test.ts`) compares the live `buildClassifierPrompt` against a LOCAL reference implementation that uses the pre-MCP-MOD-005 per-id-lookup approach; for fuzz subsets drawn in catalog declaration order, both implementations produce identical bytes. (3) The iteration-order test (`semanticPromptBuildClassifierIterationOrder.test.ts`) pins the post-refactor output order as the catalog's declaration order — explicit and reviewable, not silent. The legacy reference implementation lives ONLY inside the fuzz test file as a local function; it never leaks into the public API.
- **+8 tests / +2 suites.** Full suite **9171 tests / 340 suites — all passing** (from the 9163 / 338 baseline). Typecheck + lint + skills:validate clean.
- **Operator follow-up:** run `npx supabase functions deploy semantic-referee --linked` after merge to ship the refactored `seedPrompt.ts`. **The smoke test (`node scripts/bot-fixtures/runMcpSmokeTest.js`) is the structural regression check** — re-run it post-deploy and confirm results match the pre-MCP-MOD-005 baseline (which was 16/16 moves, 0 errors, `binaries=9` on every move per the MCP-MOD-004 post-merge KEYSTONE gate). No migration, no secret change, no admin runtime-config change. Rollback is `git revert` of the merge commit — `buildClassifierPrompt` returns to its pre-refactor per-id-lookup form.

## MCP-MOD-004 — Source-of-truth extraction: `SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG` (Epic 12 — Rules UX · Movement B)

**Status:** Build complete, awaiting Review — the KEYSTONE refactor of the modularity slate. Consolidates per-id classifier metadata (id, binarySignal, structuralQuestion, family, bannerCode, ledgerFeedbackCode, plainLanguageLabel) into a single Node + Deno catalog. `seedPrompt.ts`'s `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` is now derived (`Object.fromEntries(SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG.map(...))`) — the 23 hand-written per-id question strings are gone. The banner library's `CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS` and the ledger's `classifierFor` table stay where they are (both expose richer per-id structure — full priority list, inverse category→classifier mapping — than a single primary code; the catalog records the primary code per id and parity tests assert the agreement). **Behavior is byte-identical** — a `buildClassifierPrompt` golden snapshot captured pre-refactor matches post-refactor exactly, and every existing test still passes unchanged. **No new classifier ids, no `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_PACKET_VERSION` bump, no `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` bump, no Anthropic provider change, no inbound / outbound schema change, no admin runtime config change, no MCP adapter change, no migration, no env var.** Issue #233.

- **What it ships — new files:** `src/lib/constitution/semanticClassifierCatalog.ts` (Node-side source of truth; 23 frozen entries; pure TypeScript, same purity rules as `engine.ts`); `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/semanticClassifierCatalog.ts` (Deno-side mirror; the catalog entry literals are byte-identical to the Node file — enforced by source-text parity); `__tests__/__fixtures__/semanticClassifierCatalog/firstThreeIdsGolden.txt` (the pre-refactor `buildClassifierPrompt` output for the deterministic sample request); four new test suites: `semanticClassifierCatalogParity.test.ts` (24 tests — id-coverage, structural-question, family, banner-code, ledger-feedback-code parity, frozen-array invariants), `semanticClassifierCatalogSnapshotPrompt.test.ts` (4 tests — golden-file byte-identity + stability), `semanticClassifierCatalogDenoNodeParity.test.ts` (8 tests — source-text parity between Node + Deno catalog files for ids, families, banner codes, ledger codes, the full catalog span, and import-path conventions), `seedPromptNoHardcodedClassifierIds.test.ts` (26 tests — source-scan of `seedPrompt.ts` with comments stripped, asserts no literal per-id id-string appears in executable source for any of the 23 ids + that the file derives `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` from the catalog).
- **What it ships — modified files:** `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/seedPrompt.ts` — `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` now derives from the catalog (one-line `Object.fromEntries(...)`); the 23 hand-written per-id question entries are deleted; the worked example's classifier-id literal is sourced from `SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_CATALOG[0].id` so no per-id id-string appears in the source. `src/features/refereeBanners/classifierBannerMap.ts` — header comment documents the MCP-MOD-004 handshake (`CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS` stays because `selectBanner` consumes the full priority list, catalog records the primary code, parity test asserts agreement). `src/features/refereeLedger/reconcileMove.ts` — comment above the `classifierFor` table documents the MCP-MOD-004 handshake (table is `category → classifier`, the inverse of catalog's `id → ledgerFeedbackCode` direction; inverting it is not behaviour-preserving so the table stays; catalog records the primary feedback code per id). `docs/core/current-status.md` — this entry.
- **Doctrine encoded:** the catalog is pure TypeScript — no Supabase / React / network imports, frozen at module load, JSON-serializable values. Every `structuralQuestion` asks about the move's STRUCTURE — never about truth, popularity, person, or outcome (the existing ban-list test in `semanticAnthropicSeedPromptBanList.test.ts` scans every entry via the derived `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` and continues to pass). Every `binarySignal` is plain-language description of a structural property — never a verdict, never a person label. `bannerCode` / `ledgerFeedbackCode` are deterministic mapping anchors; user-visible language flows only through the banner library / ledger reconciliation. The catalog never authors a user-facing string itself.
- **Documented design departures (allowed by MCP-MOD-004 task spec):** (1) the catalog's `bannerCode` field is the PRIMARY code per id; the banner library's `CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS` is kept intact because `selectBanner` iterates the full ordered candidate list. (2) the catalog's `ledgerFeedbackCode` field is the PRIMARY code per id; the ledger's `classifierFor` table is kept intact because it is structurally `category → classifier` (the INVERSE direction; a single classifier can surface on multiple categories — e.g. `responds_to_parent` on both `continuity` and `direct_response`). Parity tests in `semanticClassifierCatalogParity.test.ts` assert the catalog's primary values agree with the banner library's FIRST entry per id and with the existing ledger feedback code enumeration.
- **+62 tests / +4 suites.** Full suite **9163 tests / 338 suites — all passing** (from the 9108 / 334 baseline). Typecheck + lint + skills:validate clean. The golden file at `__tests__/__fixtures__/semanticClassifierCatalog/firstThreeIdsGolden.txt` is the explicit pre-refactor byte-identity reference — touching it without a corresponding `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` bump is a doctrine violation (MCP-MOD-003 inventory §6).
- **Operator follow-up:** run `npx supabase functions deploy semantic-referee --linked` after merge to ship the Deno-side catalog. **The smoke test (`node scripts/bot-fixtures/runMcpSmokeTest.js`) is the structural regression check** — re-run it post-deploy and confirm results match the pre-refactor baseline (the unit tests are the behavioural check; the smoke test is the contract-end-to-end check). No migration, no secret change, no admin runtime-config change. Rollback is `git revert` of the merge commit — the pre-existing per-id constants were not preserved, but re-deriving them from the catalog at any commit is trivial.

## MCP-MOD-003 — Prompt template inventory (Epic 12 — Rules UX · Movement A)

**Status:** Build complete, awaiting Review — ships a single new architecture doc, `docs/architecture/semantic-referee-prompt-template.md`, that walks through the seed prompt's six layered pieces: §1 the `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_SYSTEM_PROMPT` constant verbatim with per-clause doctrine annotations naming which `cdiscourse-doctrine` / `MCP-001` rule each `You do NOT ...` clause enforces; §2 the strict-JSON contract paragraph (the `.join(' ')` of `seedPrompt.ts`'s `instruction` array) verbatim with annotations on the four-field packet contract + the inline `routeSuggestion` / `frictionSuggestion` enumerations; §3 the per-id question selection mechanism plus a fixed deterministic sample of `buildClassifierPrompt`'s full output for the first three catalog ids (`responds_to_parent`, `introduces_new_issue`, `asks_for_evidence`); §4 the redacted-input-block assembly via `buildInputBlock` plus the three-step redaction pipeline (`clientRedaction.ts` → `redaction.ts` → `seedPrompt.ts`); §5 a 19-row source-file map naming the symbol that owns each piece; §6 the `SEED_PROMPT_VERSION` stamping convention. The doc ends with an `## Invariants` section (8 properties a future change must preserve — absolute-rules list, strict-JSON wall, structural-only questions, enumerated route/friction lists, `authoritative` hard-pinned, per-call selection only, unconditional redaction, version bump on wording change) and a `## Findings` section flagging (a) one ban-list-test scope drift observation (test enforces a superset of the system prompt's named tokens), (b) two per-id questions that don't start with `Does this move ...` (`ready_for_synthesis` asks about the thread; `needs_pre_send_pause` uses `Could` and asks about the author), and (c) two duplications between the system prompt and the user-message instruction (strict-JSON contract + the `no blocking / verdict / truth / winner field` prohibition) — all informational, fed forward to MCP-MOD-005. **Documentation-only — NO production code touched.** No change to `anthropicClassifierCore.ts`, `seedPrompt.ts`, `redaction.ts`, `types.ts`, `anthropicProvider.ts`, any Edge Function, or any migration. Meta-roadmap (`docs/core/roadmap-semantic-referee-modularity.md`) updated to link the new inventory. Builds on MCP-MOD-001 (doc reorg) and feeds MCP-MOD-005 (prompt template refactor). Issue #232.

- **What it ships — new files:** `docs/architecture/semantic-referee-prompt-template.md` (six §1–§6 sections + `## Invariants` + `## Findings`), `__tests__/semanticRefereePromptTemplateParity.test.ts` (7 tests: system-prompt byte-for-byte parity, user-message-instruction byte-for-byte parity, per-id-question-sample byte-for-byte parity against `buildClassifierPrompt`'s deterministic output for a fixed input, plus three marker-presence guards and an all-three-markers structural sanity check).
- **What it ships — modified files:** `docs/core/roadmap-semantic-referee-modularity.md` (§3 row for MCP-MOD-003 now links the inventory). `docs/core/current-status.md` (this entry).
- **+7 tests / +1 suite.** Full suite **9108 tests / 334 suites — all passing**. Typecheck + lint + skills:validate clean. The parity test uses the existing `__tests__/_helpers/semanticRefereeDeno.ts` bridge to read `SEMANTIC_REFEREE_SYSTEM_PROMPT` and `buildClassifierPrompt` directly from the Deno source files; the per-id-question-sample block uses a QUADRUPLE-backtick fence in the markdown so the worked-example portion's inner ` ```json ` triple-backtick fence is preserved as literal text inside the byte-for-byte comparison.
- **No operator follow-up** — pure documentation + a source-scan test. No `db push`, no `functions deploy`, no env var, no migration, no dependency install, no `.env` change.

## MCP-MOD-002 — Classifier catalog inventory (Epic 12 — Rules UX · Movement A)

**Status:** Build complete, awaiting Review — ships a single new architecture doc, `docs/architecture/semantic-referee-classifier-catalog.md`, that walks through every one of the 23 classifier ids in catalog v0 and names, per id: the binary signal it detects (one-sentence plain-language reconstruction), the structural AI question asked of the live provider (copied byte-for-byte from `CLASSIFIER_QUESTION_TEXT` in `supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/seedPrompt.ts`), the first banner-library code listed in `CLASSIFIER_TO_BANNERS[id]` (or `—` with "intentionally silent" for `contains_unplayable_insult_only`), the `RefereeFeedbackCode` the ledger emits when the binary fires `1` for the category this id maps to in `reconcileMove.l2SignalForCategory`'s `classifierFor` table (or `—` with "not yet mapped" when no row exists), and the source-of-truth file (`supabase/functions/_shared/semanticReferee/types.ts` / `ALL_SEMANTIC_CLASSIFIER_IDS`). Family grouping follows the MCP-MOD-002 design §3: §A parent continuity (5 ids), §C evidence and source chain (7 ids), §D constructive movement (4 ids), §E debate-mode fit (3 ids), §B/§G branch routing and friction (4 ids). The doc ends with a `## Findings` section that flags 1 id with no banner mapping (the intentional silence), 12 ids with no per-id `classifierFor` ledger feedback row (they contribute through `scoreHints` or anti-amplification instead), zero wording-vs-family drift, and one near-duplicate pair (`suggests_side_branch` vs `suggests_diagonal_tangent`) — all informational, fed forward to MCP-MOD-004. **Documentation-only — NO production code touched.** No change to `seedPrompt.ts`, `types.ts`, `refereeBanners/`, `pointStanding/`, `refereeLedger/`, any Edge Function, or any migration. Meta-roadmap (`docs/core/roadmap-semantic-referee-modularity.md`) updated to link the new inventory. Builds on MCP-MOD-001 (doc reorg) and feeds MCP-MOD-004 (source-of-truth extraction). Issue #231.

- **What it ships — new files:** `docs/architecture/semantic-referee-classifier-catalog.md` (23 sections + intro + `## Findings`), `__tests__/semanticRefereeClassifierCatalogParity.test.ts` (4 tests: section-heading coverage, exactly-23 sanity, AI-question byte-for-byte parity via `<!-- ai-question:<id> -->` markers, no-missing-marker guard).
